focus: Long-loop singleton stress (100× cohort = 5,000 parses) + parse_diagnostics write-surface audit
stopCondition: continue (mechanism CONFIRMED; iter 6 should validate R-1 reset-on-throw hypothesis as Phase-2 fix)

title: Cumulative-history-in-process B2 IS REAL — surfaces between parses 383 and 433
summary: Long-loop replay of 51-file OOB cohort 100× against ONE shared web-tree-sitter@0.24.7 parser instance. Loop 1: 41 OK / 9 B1 / 0 B2 (matches iter-4 baseline). Loop 9, parse 433 (file run-all.sh): first B2 fires. Loop 100: 354 OK / 78 B1 / 4,217 B2. Heap stayed flat. Decisively confirms cumulative-history-in-process — iter 4's 51-file scope was just below threshold (≤8 traversals).

title: parse_diagnostics file_path attribution is GUARANTEED CORRECT — mis-attribution REJECTED
summary: Forward+backward call-chain trace of structural-indexer.ts:2131-2147 (for-of+await, no Promise.all, no batching) → parseFile:1219-1257 (closure-bound filePath param) → parseTreeSitter:680-756 (returns filePath:'') → attachFilePath:1246 (overwrites with closure param) → persistIndexedFileResult:547-572 (synchronous per-file) → recordParseDiagnostic:561-593 (single-row INSERT/UPSERT keyed on file_path). No async race possible. The 51-file B2 cohort is real, not propagated.

title: B2 emerges via B1 throw side-effect — bash scanner-state corruption is the trigger
summary: First B2 victim is itself a .sh file that follows ~9 cohort traversals' worth of accumulated B1 throws (missing external_scanner_reset export, see F-4.3). Mechanism inferred (medium confidence): each B1 throw escapes the WASM call without unwinding the bash external scanner state, eventually corrupting linear memory enough to OOB. Predictive corollary: parser reset on every caught throw should eliminate cumulative corruption. Iter 6 must validate via .sh-only and .sh-excluded variants.

title: Once poisoned, the singleton produces ~50 B2/loop — failure is sticky, not transient
summary: Loop 9: 1 B2. Loop 30: 717 cumulative (~50/loop steady-state). Loop 100: 4,217. Singleton transitions to corrupt state in a single bad parse and stays there for the duration; nearly every .sh attempt OOBs thereafter. Implication: 'self-heal at 5 consecutive scan-survivals' (current iter-3 plan-input) cannot heal in-scan; must be paired with parser-instance reset trigger or post-scan.

title: Heap and RSS stay flat through poisoning — B2 is NOT a memory-pressure phenomenon
summary: heapUsedMb across loops 1, 10, 30, 100 = 7.4, 8.9, 5.6, 7.9. rssMb = 3,488.3 sticky throughout. 5,000 parses in 1,746 ms (350 µs cold; faster after corruption since errors throw early). GC pressure / fragmentation / heap exhaustion are NOT the trigger — corruption is structural in WASM linear memory, not Node V8 heap. Removes 'MCP server has higher GC pressure' hypothesis from the path.

title: Housekeeping: iter-004 fixtures relocated to packet-local scratch
summary: mv operations consolidated iter-004-cohort-replay.mjs, iter-004-cohort-replay-output.txt, iter-004-026-probe-output.txt, iter-004-oob-cohort.txt from repo-root scratch/fixtures/ into the packet's scratch/fixtures/. The 0.26.8 npm-install dir (iter-004-026-probe/) was deleted as a one-shot artifact. Repo-root scratch/ removed. Packet now self-contained.
